title: announce.c: Race condition between frame_len calculation and app_data copy
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/announce.c
Lines: 544, 552, 571-574

In build_announce_frame(), the mutex is unlocked at line 544, then sched.app_data_len is read without the lock at line 552 to calculate frame_len, then the mutex is re-acquired at line 571 and app_data is copied.

If lichen_announce_sched_set_app_data() is called by another thread between lines 552 and 571, app_data_len could increase, causing memcpy at line 572 to write beyond the buffer size calculated at line 552. This is a buffer overflow vulnerability.

The fix should either:
1. Keep the mutex held while calculating frame_len, OR
2. Re-check that app_data_len hasn't increased after re-acquiring the lock
close_reason: Fixed: Race condition in build_announce_frame() by capturing app_data_len in local snapshot while holding mutex, and copying app_data from the already-captured signed_data buffer instead of re-reading from sched.app_data
